summary.decision: The documented Databricks serverless Delta surface is stable-supported.
summary.basis: Databricks adapter runtime, rendering, governance, evidence and available-now contract-runtime paths are implemented in the adapter, guarded by local tests and backed by live contract-only Kafka evidence.
summary.scope_limit: Full 1.0 GA, unbounded continuous streaming and broader Kafka provider-equivalence claims remain explicit exclusions from the stable-final claim.

real_validation_projects.name: databricks_reference_runtime_suite
real_validation_projects.required_result: Databricks adapter local suite protects the stable serverless Delta surface without importing Spark at package import time.

real_validation_projects.name: databricks_same_contract_e2e
real_validation_projects.contracts: same contracts executed on Databricks, AWS and Snowflake
real_validation_projects.required_result: User-reported same-contract E2E execution returned the same results on all three platforms.

real_validation_projects.name: databricks_confluent_kafka_available_now
real_validation_projects.required_result: First contract run consumed three Kafka messages, checkpoint rerun consumed zero rows and target/evidence readback confirmed three target rows.

accepted_review_boundaries.reason: The stable-final claim covers the documented v0.1.0 serverless Delta surface. The broader 1.0 GA gate remains governed by the Databricks GA criteria.
The stable stream claim covers checkpointed available-now contract runtime. Unbounded continuous streaming remains outside stable-final.

accepted_review_boundaries.area: Kafka provider compatibility beyond validated Confluent Cloud
accepted_review_boundaries.reason: Confluent Cloud available-now contract runtime is validated. Additional Kafka-compatible providers require separate certification before provider-equivalence claims.

do_not_claim: generic Kafka provider equivalence beyond validated Confluent Cloud
